rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF BEATRICE FORSTEIN
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Substituted Judgement For Authorization To Establish A Revocable Trust And Pour-Over Will; For Conservator To Transfer Assets Into Trust; To Appoint Trustee; For Bond; And For Court Supervision Of Trust
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing. Exhibit B to the amended petition, however, was a copy of the proposed pour-over will, not the proposed trust, which was Exhibit C. The current will NAMED the great-grandchildren and did not provide for any and all later born great-grandchildren and also provided that the beneficiaries survive by two years, but the Court accepts the allegations and requests in the amended petition. Finally, notice to a minor 12 years old or older must be given directly to the minor and also to the person having legal custody of the minor, with whom the minor resides. (There were some minor errors in the proposed trust, at pages 5 and 8.) Status of filing a copy of the order and proof of the trustee's bond in a new trust case will be set for 11/13/24. Status of filing an informational I& A in the new trust case will bet for 2/25/25. Status of filing the trustee's first account will be set for 1/20/26.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ARTIS BYRD JR
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. This matter will be called with the related cases (PES-18-301880, PES-20-304064, and PES-21-304896) involving the same real property co-owned by each estate. It should be noted that the decedent's surviving spouse, Jeanette Byrd, did not file a timely election under Probate Code section 13502(a)(1) to probate her intestate share (one-half) of the decedent's separate real property interest otherwise passing to her without administration. See sections 13500 & 13501. It is unclear whether the surviving spouse intends to file a spousal property petition or apply for court approval to make a late election under section 13502(b), or whether the personal representative intends to petition under section 13502.5 to compel a fractional interest passing to the surviving spouse to be administered with a showing of good cause required by that section. rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ARTHUR STOCKTON HOUGH JR
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The executor's attorney requests $11,115 (= $475 x 23.4 hours) for extraordinary services related to the sale of the estate real property. Statutory fees payable to the attorney for his ordinary services amount to $62,685. According to his first supplement, the attorney actually spent 108 hours on ordinary services. Based on the attorney's hourly rate ($475) used to calculate his extraordinary fees, the attorney's hourly-based fees for ordinary services actually rendered to the estate would be $51,300. See CRC 7.703(a)("The court may consider the amount of statutory compensation when determining compensation for extraordinary services"); see also Estate of Hilton (1996) 44 Cal.App.4th 890, 913 ("Finally, the amount of the ordinary fees to which such persons are entitled under the provisions of sections 901 and 910 [the then-current Probate Code sections providing for the statutory commissions of personal representatives and the statutory and extraordinary fees of their attorneys] may be considered, and if, under all the facts and circumstances bearing upon the administration of the estate, the sum allowed by law as ordinary compensation appears to be adequate, just and reasonable compensation for all services rendered, even though some extraordinary services may have in fact been performed, the probate court may, in the exercise of the discretion conferred upon it by the statute, disallow all claims for extraordinary compensation. The purpose of the Legislature in enacting sections 902 and 910 [providing for extraordinary commissions and fees of personal representatives and their attorneys] was to allow extra compensation to be granted in proper cases, but only where extraordinary services are not adequately compensated by the statutory fees established by section 901 (The court then proceeded to evaluate and reject the contention that the refusal to allow any extraordinary fees constituted an abuse of discretion.)" PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF AJAY DRALL
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/3/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) How did the personal representative determine that all net cash should be distributed to Sanjay Dilip when he leased the apartment and received rental income? [Probate Code Section 12002.] (2) Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (3) How much attorney time was spent on ordinary services? [CRC 7.703(a).] Other than the objections by Sandeep Singh Drall and the potential foreclosure action, administration of this estate was not complicated. (4) Some of the time entries described as extraordinary appear to have been ordinary, especially at the outset. This Court does not favor billing by multiple attorneys for internal conferences. Especially where attorneys and paralegals are reportedly experienced, more senior attorneys should be merely reviewing work performed by the associates and paralegals. (5) If time was billed by a paralegal, the fee declaration must include a statement addressing his/her qualifications regarding the requirements of, and compliance with, Business & Professions Code Section 6450. (6) The personal representative's relationship to the decedent does not appear to have been set forth in any pleading; he, as the person named in item 2 of the initial petition for appointment, was required to be listed in item 8 of that petition. Please provide a revised proposed order that does not have an exhibit attached after the judge's signature page and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 11/19/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. NB: In order for this Court to track closed matters, orders to terminate and distribute an estate must begin with the following language: "Order for Final Distribution." [LRSF 14.35 I.6.b.] Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and divided from the rest of the document page by a printed line. [CRC 2.110(a).] In addition to the requirements of CRC 2.111 and 7.102, all pleadings must also show the name and representative capacity, if any, of the party for whom the attorney is appearing, e.g., John Jones, Executor. "Petitioner" or "Respondent" alone is not sufficient. [LRSF 14.15 A.]

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: KLEMENS KOPEN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. (1) Need a suggestive date of death, including date, month and year. (2) Need Petition #8 The deceased ancestors through which distance generation heirs take, along with the ancestor's relationship to the decedent, when the distance generation heirs are listed in Attachment 8. The court will likely require petition for entitlement before the distribution. (3) It is not clear why the personal rep needs to be appointed unless other assets are anticipated to be probated. Petition alleges that this missing person is the surviving child of his mother who died in 2018. Petition prays the court to determine the missing person died in 2005 (5 years from 2001), which makes the missing person "predeceased" his mother, not being entitled to the distribution of the mother's estate. rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE MINNIE E. WILLIS 2003 FAMILY TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Confirming Trust Assets
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The Court is to determine whether the statute of frauds required by Probate Code section 15206 to transfer real property to a trust has been satisfied, despite the absence of any written instrument signed by the settlor AFTER the refinancing in 2006 to evidence the settlor's intent to continue to treat her real property as an asset of her 2003 trust. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF THOMAS SHONG DER
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing to admit the will. No appearance required. Status hearing for filing of a petition for final distribution or a petition for approval of report on status of administration is set for 11/18/2025.
